title: add arch linux rootless hints to install.md
body:
- related to issue #3030
- including pointer to new section in README.md provided by #3038

comments.body:
Hi @karfau. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

/ok-to-test

@karfau You must sign your PR.

git commit -a -s --amend
git push --force

@karfau Yes the failures are flakes and we will need to update your PR once they are fixed.  The problem is with changes in some of the tools doing the testing.  There was an upgrade of one, and now it is complaining about something that used to work.
CI is fixed upstream - if you can rebase and fix the signoff issue and the comment from @TomSweeneyRedHat I'd say it's good to merge

All ready, but CI still flacky?
Of course if you are checking links inside markdown, my PR would currently contain an invalid one. Nut from trying to understand the logs in the checks I couldn't find out what was the problem.

Thx for the support and let me know whenever there is something I need to do.

```
 * 714a2514 "install.md contains hints for rootless setup on arch linux" ... FAIL
  - PASS - commit does not have any whitespace errors
  - FAIL - does not have a valid DCO
```
It looks like one of your commits is missing a signoff - maybe squash them down with `git rebase`?
Ah, there's only one commit - `git commit --amend -s` and re-push then
Thx, I was very sure I did that, but maybe I missed something, will check

LGTM and all happy green test buttons.  I think this one is ready to ship.
Thanks @karfau !

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
